title: Implement provider-backed HAL power readings
description: Why this exists: t2hn.6.1 defines the board-agnostic HAL power snapshot API but intentionally leaves measured battery percent, voltage, charging, and external power unknown until concrete providers are wired. What needs to be done: add provider-backed readings using Zephyr fuel_gauge/charger/sensor/ADC APIs where available, preserve validity flags for unsupported readings, and add fake/provider tests. Acceptance criteria: no fabricated telemetry, out-of-range percent is rejected or omitted, native_sim/fake provider tests cover valid and unsupported properties, and physical board validation is tracked separately for real hardware.
notes:
Implementation slice completed for provider-backed HAL power readings. Changes: lichen_hal_power_snapshot_get now preserves voltage-divider battery voltage support and additionally reads Zephyr fuel_gauge battery percent/voltage from battery0 when the alias is a supported fuel-gauge compatible; charger-backed pmic0 now maps CHARGER_PROP_STATUS to charging and CHARGER_PROP_ONLINE to external_power. Invalid/out-of-range readings are omitted: percent >100 is invalid, voltage <=0 or >UINT16_MAX mV is invalid, unknown/out-of-range charger status and online values do not set validity flags. Kconfig/devicetree hardening now allows battery0 as voltage-divider or supported fuel gauge and asserts concrete provider drivers (VOLTAGE_DIVIDER, MAX17048, SBS_GAUGE_NEW_API, BQ27Z746; SBS_CHARGER, CHARGER_BQ24190, CHARGER_BQ25180, CHARGER_MAX20335). Added native_sim/qemu HAL provider overlay with SBS fuel-gauge and charger emulators and tests for absent/default providers, ADC voltage, fuel-gauge+charger happy path, out-of-range percent rejection, and invalid charger enum omission.

Reviews: initial Ramanujan review found P1 provider scenario test expectation failures; fixed provider-aware assertions. Initial Faraday review found P1 generic driver assertions and P2 missing positive charger mapping coverage; fixed concrete BUILD_ASSERTs and charger mapping tests. Replacement Aristotle review found P2 invalid charger enum coercion and missing invalid mapping coverage; fixed by accepting only known CHARGER_STATUS_* and CHARGER_ONLINE_* values and adding INT_MAX invalid-state tests. Final Avicenna review: no remaining P0-P2 findings. Final Newton review: no remaining P0-P2 findings.

Validation: local git diff --check passed. EC2 validation on i-069d1a57e6ac6bee6 in us-east-2a using EBS vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58, clean worktree /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-t2hn-6-1-3-validate based on e670b68 plus patch. Bootstrap hit known Ubuntu Noble awscli package issue; installed build-essential gcc g++ gcc-multilib g++-multilib libc6-dev-i386. Final command: west twister -T lichen/tests/hal -p native_sim -p qemu_x86 --outdir twister-out-t2hn-6-1-3-final --inline-logs --extra-args ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=$PWD/lichen. Result: 5 scenarios / 10 instances selected, 8 of 10 configurations passed (100%), 2 statically skipped, 0 failed, 0 errored, 0 warnings, 178 test cases executed, 82 skipped, duration 74.63s. Earlier failed Twister attempts are recorded in session context and were fixed before final pass. Physical battery/PMIC validation remains tracked by child project-LICHEN-t2hn.6.1.2.

EC2 cleanup: sync and sudo umount /mnt/lichen-zephyr completed; detached vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58 and waited until available; terminated i-069d1a57e6ac6bee6 and waited terminated; deleted temporary security group sg-02ca46d31807905b2; final volume state available with Attachments=[].
close_reason: Completed provider-backed HAL power readings with EC2 native_sim/qemu Twister evidence and two clean final reviews. Physical validation remains in project-LICHEN-t2hn.6.1.2.
